findings.description: Legacy retail rows with valid coordinates defaulted to verificationStatus=verified.
findings.failure_scenario: A pre-enrichment location with an address and coordinates but no explicit verification status is normalized and passes the new public eligibility guard, exposing an unevidenced address and map pin.

findings.description: Verified candidate merging could upgrade a same-identity row while retaining a conflicting address.
findings.failure_scenario: An existing branch with address A and an incoming verified candidate with the same branch identity but address B matches by identity; the merge keeps address A but marks it verified, publishing the wrong address.

findings.description: The SQL publication merge had the same conflicting-address upgrade path as the service merge.
findings.failure_scenario: Approval or refresh publication matches a candidate by identity when addresses differ, then upgrades the stored row to verified without changing the old address.

findings.description: Official-source evidence was accepted without proving that the source contained the extracted address.
findings.failure_scenario: The description LLM supplies an address and any official URL or unrelated official page text is present; the candidate becomes verified even though the official evidence does not mention that address.

findings.description: Candidate evidence URLs were rendered directly as admin links without an HTTP(S) scheme check.
findings.failure_scenario: A persisted candidate evidence record contains a javascript: URL; the admin review page renders it as an executable href.

findings.description: Cached SERP rows reconstructed structured entries with a second parser that omitted URL normalization and filtering.
findings.failure_scenario: A cached run passes tracking URLs or Google result URLs to location evidence handling even though a live run would strip or reject them.

findings.description: Blank Maps coordinate values were coerced to numeric zero.
findings.failure_scenario: A Maps response contains latitude or longitude as an empty string; Number('') produces 0 and downstream code can treat the place as pinned at the equator.

findings.description: Response guards accepted arrays containing malformed entries, allowing normalizers to throw inside the request handler.
findings.failure_scenario: A successful HTTP response contains places:[null] or an object without the required fields; normalization throws and the audit records a network_error without retaining the malformed response.

findings.description: The candidate publication trigger checked review overrides but not protected retail-location field state.
findings.failure_scenario: A submission is approved or a refresh is applied without a retail_locations review override while the brand field is owner/admin/submitted protected; the trigger can still merge verified candidates into the protected brand list.

findings.description: Location candidate review loading used one unbounded PostgREST IN filter.
findings.failure_scenario: An admin review page loads more than the configured filter chunk size of submissions; the candidate query URL grows beyond PostgREST/request limits while image and history queries already chunk the same IDs.

findings.description: Description-derived evidence did not carry the cached or live SERP audit row ID into the persisted candidate.
findings.failure_scenario: An admin reviews a location verified from a SERP snippet; the candidate retains the URL and excerpt but no direct audit reference, making the external-call evidence harder to trace.

findings.description: Explicit candidate acceptance matched by address or name only, so same-identity branches with a changed address could duplicate.
findings.failure_scenario: An admin accepts a candidate for a branch whose name, city, and venue match an existing row but whose address differs; the editor appends a second row instead of upgrading the existing branch.
